# Dastyare Social — CS

> Personal creator studio and social profile platform. REST API for posts and stories, with Better Auth authentication. Built with Next.js 16, PostgreSQL, and S3 media storage.

Dastyare Social CS lets creators publish threads, shorts (vertical video), media posts, and ephemeral stories. External AI agents and LLMs should use the REST API documented below.

## Documentation

- [Interactive API docs (Scalar + MCP)](/docs): Full OpenAPI reference with try-it-out and MCP tool discovery
- [OpenAPI spec (JSON)](/openapi.json): Machine-readable API contract for tool use and code generation
- [AGENTS.md](/agents.md): Detailed agent guide with endpoint examples and auth flow
- [README.md](/readme.md): Setup, architecture, and scripts

## API overview

Base URL: `{APP_URL}/api`

### Posts

- `GET /api/posts` — List posts (query: page, limit, search, type=list|count|shorts)
- `POST /api/posts` — Create post (JSON or multipart with file)
- `GET /api/posts/{id}` — Get single post
- `PATCH /api/posts/{id}` — Update post
- `DELETE /api/posts/{id}` — Delete post
- `POST /api/posts/{id}` — Actions: reaction, view
- `POST /api/posts` with `{ action: "batch-view", ids: [...] }` — Batch view increment

Post types: text, image, video, voice, file

### Stories

- `GET /api/stories` — List stories (query: page, limit, search, type=count, kind=image|video)
- `POST /api/stories` — Create story (JSON or multipart)
- `GET /api/stories/{id}` — Get single story
- `PATCH /api/stories/{id}` — Update story
- `DELETE /api/stories/{id}` — Delete story
- `POST /api/stories/{id}` — Actions: view, like (direction: inc|dec)

Story types: image, video

### Authentication

- Auth handler: `/api/auth/*` (Better Auth)
- Sign in: `POST /api/auth/sign-in/email`
- Bearer token or API key in `Authorization` header for protected operations
- Public GET endpoints do not require authentication

### Push notifications

- `POST /api/push` — Save a browser push subscription. Requires `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`.
- `POST /api/push/send` — Send browser push notifications to active subscribers. Requires `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`.
- Use this for self-hosted web push delivery to browsers that have enabled notifications.

## Optional

- tRPC endpoint at `/api/trpc` is for internal frontend use; external agents should use REST
- Content filtering (NSFW, HTML sanitization) applied on write paths
- Media uploads stored in S3-compatible object storage
